expectations.text: The response includes an ASCII diagram or structured visual (using box-drawing characters or ASCII art) that anchors the secrets management group before listing the individual decisions within it.
expectations.evidence: The output contains a clear ASCII diagram at the top using box-drawing characters (┌─┐└┘) titled 'Secrets Management Flow' showing the flow between Developer Local, CI/CD Platform, and Cloud Provider, with arrows and structured layout. This appears before the bulleted decisions list.

expectations.text: The decisions listed are stated as straightforward assumed defaults (one-line statements of the decided approach) — they do NOT include ⭐/❌ tradeoff analysis or multiple competing options.
expectations.evidence: All six decisions are presented as declarative statements of the chosen approach: 'Build-time secrets stored in GitHub Secrets', 'Deploy-time secrets stored in GitHub Secrets', 'Runtime secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager', etc. None include ⭐/❌ symbols, competing options, or tradeoff analysis.

expectations.text: The response ends with exactly two options: one to confirm and proceed, one to type a correction.
expectations.evidence: The output ends with exactly two numbered options: '1. ✅ Accept and move to Group 4 (CI workflow structure)' and '2. ✏️ Type a correction'
